verdicts.reason: The Named Snapshots feature correctly handles storage limits, data corruption, and user input, adhering to existing architectural constraints without introducing new correctness risks.
The Named Snapshots feature adheres to the project's established security model and architectural constraints, introducing no new attack surfaces or data exposure beyond the baseline.
The Named Snapshots feature provides a clear user flow with good affordances, appropriate feedback, and addresses previous mobile/accessibility concerns.
The Named Snapshots feature is highly discoverable through its toolbar button, tooltip, keyboard shortcut, and clear in-app instructions within the modal and documentation.
Named snapshots provide essential version control, significantly enhancing the utility and trustworthiness of Markdown Deck as a serious content creation tool.
verdicts.evidence: Version history/snapshots are standard features in professional document editors (e.g., Google Docs, Word, Figma) and directly address the critical user need to manage iterations and prevent loss of work beyond simple auto-save.

verdicts.reason: The feature provides a surprisingly rich and visually intuitive local version history, complete with thumbnails and named checkpoints, within the strict constraints of a single-file, zero-dependency HTML application. This elevates it beyond a generic 'undo history' and makes it a signature utility for this unique tool.
The Named Snapshots feature adheres to established patterns for modal width, local storage, DOM rendering, and UI element handling, as documented in prior learnings and structural constraints.
